title: The Lessons of the Lewis and Clark Expedition
author: From: The Journals of Meriwether Lewis
content: The expedition succeeded not because of a rigid plan, but because of constant, daily adaptation to the unmapped landscape. Planning is an illusion; preparation for the unknown is a strategy.

title: The Strategy of the Underground Railroad
author: From: Historical Networks
content: True resilience in complex systems comes from decentralized, loosely coupled networks. They are impossible to break because there is no single point of failure.
action: Diversify project support and information sources.

title: The Industrial Pragmatism of Benjamin Franklin
author: From: The Autobiography
content: Franklin treated his character like an accounting ledger, tracking virtues and failures daily. He understood that improvement is a process of data-driven iteration.

title: The Law of Unintended Consequences
author: From: The Prohibition Era
content: When you force a complex system into a rigid state, it creates a shadow system that is often worse than the original problem.
action: Review rigid rules for shadow behaviors.

title: The Frontier Resilience
author: From: The Oregon Trail
content: The pioneers who survived were those who prioritized 'utility' over 'prestige.' They abandoned heavy, beautiful possessions to save the oxen.
action: Drop one heavy, non-essential task or tool.
